fields.name: Worksheet Template Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the requisition worksheet template.

fields.name: Journal Batch Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the journal batch, a personalized journal layout, that the entries were posted from.

fields.name: Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the requisition worksheet line.

fields.name: Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of requisition worksheet line you are creating.

fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the involved entry or record, according to the specified number series.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies a description of the purchase order.

fields.name: Description 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies additional text describing the entry, or a remark about the requisition worksheet line.

fields.name: Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity to be purchased.

fields.name: Vendor No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the vendor who will ship the items in the purchase order.

fields.name: Direct Unit Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies the cost of one unit of the selected item or resource.

fields.name: Due Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when you can expect to receive the items.

fields.name: Requester ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ID of the user who is ordering the items on the line.

fields.name: Confirmed
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the items on the line have been approved for purchase.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 1, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 2, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Location Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a code for an inventory location where the items that are being ordered will be registered.

fields.name: Recurring Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies a recurring method, if you have indicated in the Recurring field that the worksheet is a recurring requisition worksheet.

fields.name: Expiration Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the last date on which the recurring requisition worksheet will be converted to a purchase order.

fields.name: Recurring Frequency
fields.tooltip: Specifies a recurring frequency, if it is indicated in the Recurring field that the worksheet is a recurring requisition worksheet.

fields.name: Order Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the order date that will apply to the requisition worksheet line.

fields.name: Vendor Item No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number that the vendor uses for this item.

fields.name: Sales Order No.
fields.tooltip:
Shows the number of the sales order for which the items on the purchase line will be ordered if the line is a drop shipment.
The program retrieves the number automatically from the sales line when you choose Functions, Drop Shipment, Get Sales Orders.
When the requisition worksheet line is converted to a purchase order, the items on the purchase order line will still be marked as a drop shipment, which is shipped directly from the vendor to the customer.

fields.name: Sales Order Line No.
fields.tooltip:
Shows the number of the sales order line for which the items on the purchase line will be ordered if the requisition worksheet line is a drop shipment.
The program retrieves the number automatically from the sales line when you choose Functions, Drop Shipment, Get Sales Orders.
When the requisition worksheet line is converted to a purchase order, the items on the purchase order line will still be marked as a drop shipment, which is shipped directly from the vendor to the customer.

fields.name: Sell-to Customer No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the customer.

fields.name: Ship-to Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a code for an alternate shipment address if you want to ship to another address than the one that has been entered automatically. This field is also used in case of drop shipment.

fields.name: Order Address Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the order address of the related vendor.

fields.name: Currency Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the currency code for the requisition lines.

fields.name: Currency Factor
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Reserved Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of this item have been reserved.

fields.name: Purchaser Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies which purchaser is assigned to the vendor.

fields.name: Drop Shipment
fields.tooltip: Specifies if demand is for drop shipment or not.

fields.name: Dimension Set ID
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a reference to a combination of dimension values. The actual values are stored in the Dimension Set Entry table.


Additional Information


The dimension set ID is not edited directly by users. The value of the field is calculated with the dimension values that users select in the Edit Dimension Set Entries window.


The value 0 represents an empty dimension set.

fields.name: Variant Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the variant of the item on the line.

fields.name: Bin Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the bin of the item on the line.

fields.name: Qty. per Unit of Measure
fields.tooltip: If you have filled in the Purch. Unit of Measure field on the item card and entered a quantity for the current item in the Qty. of Base Unit of Measure per Unit field, the program automatically fills in this field when you enter the item number on the line.

fields.name: Unit of Measure Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies how each unit of the item or resource is measured, such as in pieces or hours. By default, the value in the Base Unit of Measure field on the item or resource card is inserted.

fields.name: Quantity (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies that when the quantity field is updated, this field is updated.

fields.name: Reserved Qty. (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies that when the reserved quantity field is updated, this field is updated.

fields.name: Demand Type
fields.tooltip:
Specifies what type of demand that the planning line in the Order Planning window is created for.
The field is only filled for expandable planning lines that represent the header of orders that create demand.
The following demand types exist:


Demand type


Description


Production

Represents a production order. This expands to show one or more component lines that have insufficient availability.


Sales

Represents a sales order or negative return order. This expands to show one or more sales order lines that have insufficient availability.


Service

Represents a service order. This expands to show one or more service order lines on the sales order that have insufficient availability.


Job

Represents a job. This expands to show one or more job planning lines that have insufficient availability.


Assembly

Represents an assembly order. This expands to show one or more assembly order lines that have insufficient availability.

fields.name: Demand Subtype
fields.tooltip: Specifies the demand subtype that the planning line is related to. For example, if the planning line is related to a sales order line, then the value in the field is copied from the Document Type field on the sales order line.

fields.name: Demand Order No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the demanded order that represents the planning line.

fields.name: Demand Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line number of the demand, such as a sales order line.

fields.name: Demand Ref. No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a reference number for the document line that represents the demand on the planning line, such as a sales order line.
For example, the value in the field is copied from the Line No. field of the sales order line.

fields.name: Status
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the status of the demand order and is only filled for expandable planning lines representing demand orders. The field can show two sets of status types, depending on whether the planning line is for a sales order or a production order.
Lines that represent sales demand can show a status of Open or Released - copied from the sales order.
Lines that represent production demand can show a status of Planned, Firm Planned, or Released - copied from the production order.

fields.name: Demand Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the demanded date of the demand that the planning line represents.

fields.name: Demand Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity needed relating to the purchase order line item.

fields.name: Demand Quantity (Base)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the quantity of the demand that the planning line represents. This quantity is expressed in the base unit of measure.
If the demand is a sales line, then it is the value in the Quantity (Base) field of that sales line. If the demand is a component line, then it is the value in the Expected Quantity (Base) field of that component line.
These fields are used to calculate the needed quantity using the following formula:
Demand Quantity - Demand Qty. Available = Needed Quantity

fields.name: Needed Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies the demand quantity that is not available and must be ordered to meet the demand represented on the planning line.

fields.name: Needed Quantity (Base)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the quantity of the demand quantity that is not available. This is the quantity that must be ordered to meet the demand that the planning line represents. The quantity is expressed in the base unit of measure and is calculated as follows:
Demand Quantity - Demand Qty. Available = Needed Quantity

fields.name: Reserve
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the item on the planning line has a setting of Always in the Reserve field on its item card.

fields.name: Qty. per UOM (Demand)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the quantity per unit of measure of the item on an expandable line in the Order Planning window.
Expandable lines represent headers of the orders that create the demand.
For more information about the types of demand that are supported, see Demand Type.

fields.name: Unit Of Measure Code (Demand)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the unit of measure of the item on an expandable line in the Order Planning window.
Expandable lines represent headers of the orders that create the demand.
For more information about the types of demand that are supported, see Demand Type.

fields.name: Supply From
fields.tooltip: Specifies a value, according to the selected replenishment system, before a supply order can be created for the line.

fields.name: Original Item No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the item that was originally on the component list before it was substituted.

fields.name: Original Variant Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the variant that was originally on the component list before it was substituted.

fields.name: Level
fields.tooltip: Specifies line indentation in the Description field.

fields.name: Demand Qty. Available
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many of the demand quantity are available.

fields.name: User ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ID of the user who last modified the planning line.

fields.name: Item Category Code
fields.tooltip:
Contains the item category code for the item.
You can use item categories to group default item setup values to make creating new items easier. An item category code specifies the following values:

Default general product posting group.


Default inventory posting group.


Default VAT product posting group.


Default costing method.


To see or select item categories in the Item Category window, click the drop-down arrow in the field.

fields.name: Nonstock
fields.tooltip:
Specifies if the item on the line is a nonstock item.
Nonstock items are items that are not usually kept in inventory.

fields.name: Purchasing Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for a special procurement method, such as drop shipment.

fields.name: Transfer-from Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the location that items are transferred from.

fields.name: Transfer Shipment Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the shipment date of the transfer order proposal.

fields.name: Price Calculation Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies the method that will be used for price calculation in the requisition line.

fields.name: Line Discount %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the discount percentage used to calculate the purchase line discount.

fields.name: Blanket Purch. Order Exists
fields.tooltip: Specifies if a blanket purchase order exists for the item on the requisition line.

fields.name: MPS Order
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the requisition worksheet line is an MPS order, that is, whether it is linked to a demand forecast or a sales order.

fields.name: Planning Flexibility
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the supply, represented by the requisition worksheet line, is considered by the planning system, when calculating action messages.

fields.name: Routing Reference No.
fields.tooltip:
This field is used internally. The routing reference number corresponds to the routing reference number of the production order line that the routing is related to.
The program displays a zero in this field when the routing line is related to a family order where more production order lines share the same routing.

fields.name: Gen. Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the item's product type to link transactions made for this item with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

fields.name: Gen. Business Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the general business posting group to be used for the item when you post the planning worksheet.

fields.name: Low-Level Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the planning level of this item entry in the planning worksheet.

fields.name: Original Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity stated on the production or purchase order, when an action message proposes to change the quantity on an order.

fields.name: Finished Quantity
fields.tooltip:
Specifies how much of the quantity on the production order line has been produced.
The program copies this information from the existing production order.

fields.name: Remaining Quantity
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the difference between the finished quantity and the planned quantity or shows 0 if the finished quantity is greater than the remaining quantity.
The program copies this information from the existing production order.

fields.name: Original Due Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the due date stated on the production or purchase order, when an action message proposes to reschedule an order.

fields.name: Starting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the starting date of the manufacturing process, if the planned supply is a production order.

fields.name: Starting Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the starting time of the manufacturing process.

fields.name: Ending Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ending date of the manufacturing process, if the planned supply is a production order.

fields.name: Ending Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ending time for the manufacturing process.

fields.name: Indirect Cost %
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the indirect costs as a percentage.
The program automatically retrieves the indirect cost % from the Item table when the No. field is filled in, but you can change it.
You can specify an indirect cost percentage. An indirect cost can represent freight costs associated with the purchased item. It can also represent inventory carrying costs. The percentage is added to the direct cost of the item. Indirect cost is included in the calculation of the contents of the Standard Cost field on the item card.

fields.name: Overhead Rate
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the overhead rate.
The program automatically retrieves the overhead rate from the Item table, when the No. field is filled in, but you can change it.
The overhead rate is part of the standard cost calculation. The overhead rate can represent transportation costs associated with the purchased item. It can also represent inventory carrying costs. The amount is added to the direct cost of the item.
The overhead rate is included in the calculation of the contents of the Standard Cost field on the item card.

fields.name: Unit Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies the cost of one unit of the item or resource on the line.

fields.name: Cost Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total costs for the requisition worksheet line.

fields.name: Replenishment System
fields.tooltip: Specifies which kind of order to use to create replenishment orders and order proposals.

fields.name: Ref. Order No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the relevant production or purchase order.

fields.name: Ref. Order Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the order is a purchase order, a production order, or a transfer order.

fields.name: Ref. Order Status
fields.tooltip: Specifies the status of the production order.

fields.name: Ref. Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the purchase or production order line.

fields.name: No. Series
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the number series that is linked to the planning line. The program uses the number series when it creates proposals for new production orders. It is the same number series that is used for planned production orders.
To see the codes that have been set up in the No. Series table, click the field.

fields.name: Expected Component Cost Amt.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the expected cost amount of the component.
The program calculates the amount in this field as the expected component costs multiplied by quantity.

fields.name: Finished Qty. (Base)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies how much of the quantity on the production order line, stated in base units of measure, has been produced.
The program automatically updates this field when you post final routing list operations from the output journal.

fields.name: Remaining Qty. (Base)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the difference between the finished quantity and the planned quantity or shows 0 if the finished quantity is greater than the remaining quantity. The value is stated in base units of measure.
The program automatically calculates this information from the Remaining Quantity and Qty. per Unit of Measure fields.

fields.name: Related to Planning Line
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Planning Level
fields.tooltip: Indicates the planning level of the item in multi-level production orders. The planning level is calculated only for items that have Make-to-Order specified in the Manufacturing Policy field.

fields.name: Planning Line Origin
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the origin of the planning line. The options are Action Message, Planning and Blank.
A blank indicates that the planning line was entered manually.

fields.name: Action Message
fields.tooltip: Specifies an action to take to rebalance the demand-supply situation.

fields.name: Accept Action Message
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to accept the action message proposed for the line.

fields.name: Net Quantity (Base)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the quantity, in base units, that the planning line contributes to the item's availability.
The program automatically calculates the field as the difference between the Original Quantity and Quantity fields multiplied by the quantity per unit of measure.

fields.name: Starting Date-Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the starting date and the starting time, which are combined in a format called "starting date-time".

fields.name: Ending Date-Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ending date and the ending time, which are combined in a format called "ending date-time".

fields.name: Order Promising ID
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Order Promising Line No.
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Order Promising Line ID
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.
